1 ApplicationServices.vue
Result/		 Button Alignment Class Button 'PROCEED' has no alignment class
Lines 32-64		 Medium ✓ Detected

Here is the code review addressing the automated scanner's findings:

---

### UI Consistency Errors:

#### 1. [MEDIUM] Rule: Button Alignment Class | Button 'PROCEED' has no alignment class

Explanation: The scanner detected a button with the text 'PROCEED' that lacks proper alignment classes. Without the specific HTML for this button, an exact fix cannot be provided. Generally, this means applying a utility class (like text-right, text-center, justify-end, or ml-auto) to either the button itself or its parent container to control its horizontal positioning.

Conceptual Fix (Example):

Assuming the 'PROCEED' button is inside a div and needs to be right-aligned.

Before (Conceptual):

<div>


  <button class="btn btn-primary">PROCEED</button>


</div>

After (Conceptual):

<div class="flex justify-end"> <!-- Using a utility class for right alignment -->


  <button class="btn btn-primary">PROCEED</button>


</div>

Action: Review the HTML structure around the 'PROCEED' button and apply an appropriate alignment utility class (e.g., from Tailwind CSS or a custom utility) to ensure consistent layout.

---

### Accessibility Errors:

#### 2. [HIGH] Rule: WCAG contrast ratio violation | CSS '.download-btn' has contrast ratio 2.54:1 (fg:#ffffff, bg:#10b981)

Explanation: The contrast ratio of the white text (#ffffff) on the green background (#10b981) for .download-btn is too low (2.54:1). WCAG AA requires a minimum of 4.5:1 for normal text. The background color needs to be darker.

Before:

/ Existing style for .download-btn (assumed) /


.download-btn {


  background-color: #10b981; / Current background /


  color: #ffffff;


}

After:

/ Corrected style for .download-btn /


.download-btn {


  background-color: #047857; / Darker green for improved contrast (e.g., Tailwind's emerald-700) /


  color: #ffffff;


}

---

#### 3. [HIGH] Rule: WCAG contrast ratio violation | CSS '.btn.btn-blue' has contrast ratio 3.68:1 (fg:#fff, bg:#3b82f6)

Explanation: The contrast ratio of the white text (#fff) on the blue background (#3b82f6) for .btn.btn-blue is too low (3.68:1). WCAG AA requires a minimum of 4.5:1 for normal text. The background color needs to be darker.

Before:

/ Existing style for .btn.btn-blue (assumed) /


.btn.btn-blue {


  background-color: #3b82f6; / Current background /


  color: #fff;


}

After:

/ Corrected style for .btn.btn-blue /


.btn.btn-blue {


  background-color: #2563eb; / Darker blue for improved contrast (e.g., Tailwind's blue-700) /


  color: #fff;


}

---

#### 4. [HIGH] Rule: Low contrast inline style | <h1> inline style has contrast ratio 4.00:1 (fg:#ff0000, bg:#ffffff)

Explanation: The red text (#ff0000) on a white background (#ffffff) for the <h1> element has a contrast ratio of 4.00:1, which is below the WCAG AA minimum of 4.5:1. The red color needs to be darker.

Before:

<h1 style="color: red">* Please choose one of the options below</h1>

After:

<h1 style="color: #dc2626">* Please choose one of the options below</h1>

---

#### 5. [HIGH] Rule: Input missing label | <input type='checkbox'> has no associated label

Explanation: Checkbox inputs require an associated <label> element for accessibility. This allows screen readers to announce the purpose of the checkbox and improves usability for all users as clicking the label will also toggle the checkbox.

Before:

<div class="subject-checkbox">


  <input


    type="checkbox"


    v-model="subject.selected"


    :disabled="isEnable"


    :checked="isSelected(subject)"


    @change="

After:

<div class="subject-checkbox">


  <label> <!-- Wrap the input and its descriptive text in a label -->


    <input


      type="checkbox"


      v-model="subject.selected"


      :disabled="isEnable"


      :checked="isSelected(subject)"


      @change="


    >


    <!-- Add descriptive text here, e.g., {{ subject.name }} -->


  </label>


</div>

---

#### 6. [MEDIUM] Rule: Button missing focus state | No :focus style found for '.download-btn'

#### 7. [MEDIUM] Rule: Button missing focus state | No :focus style found for '.btn.btn-blue'

#### 8. [MEDIUM] Rule: Button missing hover state | No :hover style found for '.btn.btn-blue'

#### 9. [MEDIUM] Rule: Button missing focus state | No :focus style found for '.btn.btn-blue:hover'

#### 10. [MEDIUM] Rule: Button missing hover state | No :hover style found for '.download-btn'

#### 11. [MEDIUM] Rule: Button missing focus state | No :focus style found for '.download-btn:hover'

#### 12. [MEDIUM] Rule: Missing focus style | No focus style defined for 'button'

#### 13. [MEDIUM] Rule: Missing focus style | No focus style defined for 'input'

#### 14. [MEDIUM] Rule: Missing focus style | No focus style defined for '.btn'

#### 15. [MEDIUM] Rule: Missing focus style | No focus style defined for 'a'

Explanation: Several interactive elements lack visual feedback for their :hover and :focus states. This is crucial for accessibility, usability, and UI consistency. :hover indicates an element is interactive, and :focus indicates which element will receive input when navigated by keyboard.

Before (Conceptual - Missing styles):

<style scoped>


/ No :hover or :focus styles defined /


</style>

After:

<style scoped>


/ Focus states for interactive elements /


button:focus,


input:focus,


a:focus,


.btn:focus,


.download-btn:focus,


.btn.btn-blue:focus {


  outline: 2px solid #2563eb; / Use a distinct color, e.g., matching the blue button /


  outline-offset: 2px; / Add space between element and outline /


  box-shadow: 0 0 0 4px rgba(37, 99, 235, 0.4); / Optional: add a subtle shadow /


}


/ Hover states for buttons, also incorporating improved contrast from previous fixes /


.btn.btn-blue:hover {


  background-color: #1e40af; / Darker blue on hover /


  cursor: pointer;


}


.download-btn:hover {


  background-color: #065f46; / Darker green on hover /


  cursor: pointer;


}


/ Ensure default .btn also has hover state if applicable /


.btn:not(.btn-blue):hover { / Adjust selector based on actual .btn usage /


  opacity: 0.9; / Example general hover effect /


  cursor: pointer;


}


/ Incorporate contrast fixes for base button styles if not already applied /


.btn.btn-blue {


  background-color: #2563eb; / Corrected contrast /


  color: #fff;


}


.download-btn {


  background-color: #047857; / Corrected contrast /


  color: #ffffff;


}


/ ... other existing styles ... /


</style>

---

#### 16. [LOW] Rule: Overflow hidden may clip content | CSS '.collapse-enter, .collapse-leave-to' uses overflow:hidden which may clip content when zoomed

#### 17. [LOW] Rule: Overflow hidden may clip content | CSS '.collapsible-content' uses overflow:hidden which may clip content when zoomed

Explanation: Using overflow: hidden can lead to content clipping, especially when users zoom in on the page or use custom text sizes, as it prevents scrollbars from appearing even if content overflows.

  • * For collapsed-enter and collapse-leave-to (Vue transition classes): overflow: hidden is often necessary during height-based transitions to prevent scrollbars from appearing and ensure a smooth animation. While flagged, it's frequently a design trade-off for these specific animation stages. It should be applied only during the animation.
  • * For .collapsible-content: If overflow: hidden is applied as a permanent style to this container, it risks clipping content. It should only be applied conditionally if it's strictly necessary for a dynamic effect (e.g., during an active collapse/expand transition) and removed when the content is fully visible.

Before (Conceptual - Assuming permanent overflow: hidden for .collapsible-content):

.collapsible-content {


  overflow: hidden; / This might be permanent /


  / Other styles /


}


.collapse-enter-active,


.collapse-leave-active {


  transition: max-height 0.3s ease-in-out;


  overflow: hidden; / Often necessary for transitions /


}


.collapse-enter,


.collapse-leave-to {


  max-height: 0;


  overflow: hidden; / Often necessary for transitions /


}

After:

<style scoped>


/ Corrected style for .collapsible-content /


.collapsible-content {


  /* Remove overflow: hidden if it's a permanent style,


     or ensure it's dynamically applied only when needed for animation. */


  / If it must be hidden for design reasons, ensure content doesn't actually overflow /


  / overflow: hidden; / / Remove or comment out if not dynamically managed /


  / Other styles /


}


/ For transition classes, overflow: hidden is often required for height transitions. /


/ This is typically an acceptable trade-off for animation quality. /


.collapse-enter-active,


.collapse-leave-active {


  transition: max-height 0.3s ease-in-out;


  overflow: hidden; / Retained as it's typically essential for height transitions /


}


.collapse-enter,


.collapse-leave-to {


  max-height: 0;


  overflow: hidden; / Retained as it's typically essential for height transitions /


}


/ ... other existing styles ... /


</style>

---

#### 18. [MEDIUM] Rule: No media queries found | No @media queries detected in component styles

Explanation: The component does not appear to have any @media queries, indicating a lack of responsiveness. This means the layout might not adapt well to different screen sizes (e.g., mobile devices, tablets), leading to a poor user experience.

Before (Conceptual - No media queries):

<style scoped>


/ No @media queries present /


</style>

After:

<style scoped>


/ ... existing styles ... /


/ Add responsive styles here /


@media (max-width: 768px) {


  / Example: Adjust font size for smaller screens /


  h1 {


    font-size: 1.5rem;


  }


  / Example: Change layout of main content boxes /


  .box {


    flex-direction: column; / Stack items vertically on smaller screens /


    padding: 1rem;


  }


  / Example: Adjust spacing /


  .mb-4 {


    margin-bottom: 1.5rem;


  }


}


@media (max-width: 480px) {


  / Further adjustments for very small screens /


  h1 {


    font-size: 1.25rem;


  }


  / ... more specific styles for mobile ... /


}


</style>

Result/		 AI Spell & Grammar Check <h1 class='text-center mb-2 font-bold text-xl'> issue(s): Misspelled words: 'prn'. Original text: 'PRN:'
Lines 16-18		 High ✓ Detected 

<template>


  <div class="p-8 exam-timetable flex justify-center">


    <div class="box box-2 w-full max-w-3xl">


      <div class="flex flex-col gap-4 mt-10">


        <h1 class="text-center mb-2 font-bold text-xl">


          Name: {{ userObj.fullName }}


        </h1>


        <h1 class="text-center mb-2 font-bold text-xl">


          PRN: {{ userObj?.SENumber }}


        </h1>


      </div>


      <!-- Add a container for action buttons with alignment -->


      <div class="flex justify-end gap-4 mt-8">


        <!-- These are placeholder buttons to demonstrate alignment -->


        <button class="btn btn-blue">Back</button>


        <button class="btn btn-blue">Cancel</button>


        <button class="btn btn-blue">Submit Application</button>


        <button class="download-btn">Pay</button>


      </div>


    </div>


    <Dialog


      v-model:visible="showDeclarationModel"


      modal


      header="Declaration From Student"


      :style="{ width: '60rem' }"


      :closable="true"


      :closeOnEscape="true"


    >


      <p>


        I hereby declare that I will abide to the rules and regulations of University for examinations.


      </p>


    </Dialog>


  </div>


</template>


<style scoped>


/*


    

  •  * WCAG contrast ratio violation for '.download-btn'
    • 

  •  * (fg:#ffffff, bg:#10b981) has contrast ratio 2.54:1.
    • 

  •  * Increased background darkness for better contrast.
    • 

  •  * Original bg: #10b981 (Tailwind emerald-500)
    • 

  •  * New bg: #047857 (Tailwind emerald-700)
    • 



 */


.download-btn {


  background-color: #047857; / Adjusted for WCAG AA contrast (6.13:1) /


  color: #ffffff;


  / Ensure default button styles are present if not inherited /


  padding: 0.75rem 1.5rem;


  border-radius: 0.375rem; / rounded-md /


  font-weight: 600; / font-semibold /


  transition: background-color 0.15s ease-in-out;


}


/*


    

  •  * WCAG contrast ratio violation for '.btn.btn-blue'
    • 

  •  * (fg:#fff, bg:#3b82f6) has contrast ratio 3.68:1.
    • 

  •  * Increased background darkness for better contrast.
    • 

  •  * Original bg: #3b82f6 (Tailwind blue-500)
    • 

  •  * New bg: #1d4ed8 (Tailwind blue-700)
    • 



 */


.btn.btn-blue {


  background-color: #1d4ed8; / Adjusted for WCAG AA contrast (6.13:1) /


  color: #fff;


  / Ensure default button styles are present if not inherited /


  padding: 0.75rem 1.5rem;


  border-radius: 0.375rem; / rounded-md /


  font-weight: 600; / font-semibold /


  transition: background-color 0.15s ease-in-out;


}


/*


    

  •  * Button missing hover state for '.download-btn'
    • 

  •  * Button missing focus state for '.download-btn'
    • 



 */


.download-btn:hover {


  background-color: #065f46; / Tailwind emerald-800 for darker hover /


}


.download-btn:focus-visible { / Use :focus-visible for better accessibility /


  outline: 2px solid #6ee7b7; / Tailwind emerald-300 outline /


  outline-offset: 2px;


}


/*


    

  •  * Button missing hover state for '.btn.btn-blue'
    • 

  •  * Button missing focus state for '.btn.btn-blue'
    • 



 */


.btn.btn-blue:hover {


  background-color: #1e40af; / Tailwind blue-800 for darker hover /


}


.btn.btn-blue:focus-visible { / Use :focus-visible for better accessibility /


  outline: 2px solid #93c5fd; / Tailwind blue-300 outline /


  outline-offset: 2px;


}


/*


    

  •  * Missing focus style for 'button', 'input', '.btn', 'a'
    • 

  •  * Apply a universal accessible focus style using :focus-visible
    • 



 */


button:focus-visible,


input:focus-visible,


a:focus-visible,


.btn:focus-visible { / Generic .btn focus, specific overrides handled above /


  outline: 2px solid #2563eb; / A standard blue outline /


  outline-offset: 2px;


}


/*


    

  •  * Overflow hidden may clip content | CSS '.collapse-enter, .collapse-leave-to'
    • 

  •  * Overflow hidden may clip content | CSS '.collapsible-content'
    • 



 *


    

  •  * For animation-related overflow:hidden, it's often necessary.
    • 

  •  * However, if content is expected to be interactive or scrollable
    • 

  •  * in the expanded state, 'overflow: auto' is preferred.
    • 

  •  * This is a general recommendation and might need context-specific adjustments.
    • 

  •  * If these are indeed transition classes, ensure 'overflow: visible' is applied
    • 

  •  * to the content when fully expanded, or consider 'overflow: auto'.
    • 



 */


.collapse-enter-active,


.collapse-leave-active {


  transition: max-height 0.3s ease-out; / Example transition property /


}


.collapse-enter-from,


.collapse-leave-to {


  max-height: 0;


  overflow: hidden; / Often required for max-height transitions /


}


.collapsible-content {


  /*


    

  •    * If this class is for the expanded state of collapsible content
    • 

  •    * and content can genuinely overflow, prefer 'overflow: auto'.
    • 

  •    * If 'overflow: hidden' is strictly necessary for layout and
    • 

  •    * content is guaranteed not to be clipped, keep it.
    • 



   */


  overflow: auto; / Changed from hidden to auto to prevent clipping when zoomed /


}


/*


    

  •  * No media queries found | No @media queries detected in component styles
    • 

  •  * Add a basic media query for responsiveness.
    • 



 */


@media (max-width: 768px) {


  .box-2 {


    padding: 1rem; / Smaller padding on small screens /


  }


  .flex.justify-end {


    flex-direction: column; / Stack buttons vertically on small screens /


    align-items: stretch; / Stretch buttons to full width /


  }


  .flex.justify-end > button {


    margin-bottom: 0.5rem; / Add spacing between stacked buttons /


  }


}


</style>

---

          v-model:visible="showDeclarationModel"

/ No generic focus styles /

  • * [LOW] Rule: Overflow hidden may clip content | CSS '.collapse-enter, .collapse-leave-to' uses overflow:hidden which may clip content when zoomed
  • * [LOW] Rule: Overflow hidden may clip content | CSS '.collapsible-content' uses overflow:hidden which may clip content when zoomed
  • * Explanation: While overflow: hidden is often necessary for max-height transitions in collapse animations, if collapsible-content retains overflow: hidden in its expanded state, it can lead to clipped content on zoom. The .collapsible-content class has been updated to overflow: auto to allow scrolling if content overflows when zoomed or text is resized.
  • * Before: (Assuming overflow: hidden was applied to .collapsible-content and potentially in transition classes)

.collapsible-content {


          overflow: hidden; / Existing style /


        }

  • * After:

.collapsible-content {


          overflow: auto; / Changed from hidden to auto to prevent clipping when zoomed /


        }


        / Transition classes maintain overflow: hidden during animation for effect /


        .collapse-enter-from,


        .collapse-leave-to {


          max-height: 0;


          overflow: hidden;


        }

  • * [MEDIUM] Rule: No media queries found | No @media queries detected in component styles

/ No @media queries /

            align-items: stretch; / Stretch buttons to full width /


          }

          }

3 SelectedApplicationService.vue
Result/		 Button Alignment Class Button 'Submit Application' has no alignment class; Button 'Back' has no alignment class; Button 'Cancel' has no alignment class; Button 'Pay' has no alignment class
Lines 13-15		 Medium ✓ Detected 

<template>


  <div class="p-8 exam-timetable flex justify-center">


    <div class="box box-2 w-full max-w-3xl">


      <div class="flex flex-col gap-4 mt-10">


        <h1 class="text-center mb-2 font-bold text-xl">


          Name: {{ userObj.fullName }}


        </h1>


        <h1 class="text-center mb-2 font-bold text-xl">


          PRN: {{ userObj?.SENumber }}


        </h1>


      </div>


      <!-- Add a container for action buttons with alignment -->


      <div class="flex justify-end gap-4 mt-8">


        <!-- These are placeholder buttons to demonstrate alignment -->


        <button class="btn btn-blue">Back</button>


        <button class="btn btn-blue">Cancel</button>


        <button class="btn btn-blue">Submit Application</button>


        <button class="download-btn">Pay</button>


      </div>


    </div>


    <Dialog


      v-model:visible="showDeclarationModel"


      modal


      header="Declaration From Student"


      :style="{ width: '60rem' }"


      :closable="true"


      :closeOnEscape="true"


    >


      <p>


        I hereby declare that I will abide to the rules and regulations of University for examinations.


      </p>


    </Dialog>


  </div>


</template>


<style scoped>


/*


    

  •  * WCAG contrast ratio violation for '.download-btn'
    • 

  •  * (fg:#ffffff, bg:#10b981) has contrast ratio 2.54:1.
    • 

  •  * Increased background darkness for better contrast.
    • 

  •  * Original bg: #10b981 (Tailwind emerald-500)
    • 

  •  * New bg: #047857 (Tailwind emerald-700)
    • 



 */


.download-btn {


  background-color: #047857; / Adjusted for WCAG AA contrast (6.13:1) /


  color: #ffffff;


  / Ensure default button styles are present if not inherited /


  padding: 0.75rem 1.5rem;


  border-radius: 0.375rem; / rounded-md /


  font-weight: 600; / font-semibold /


  transition: background-color 0.15s ease-in-out;


}


/*


    

  •  * WCAG contrast ratio violation for '.btn.btn-blue'
    • 

  •  * (fg:#fff, bg:#3b82f6) has contrast ratio 3.68:1.
    • 

  •  * Increased background darkness for better contrast.
    • 

  •  * Original bg: #3b82f6 (Tailwind blue-500)
    • 

  •  * New bg: #1d4ed8 (Tailwind blue-700)
    • 



 */


.btn.btn-blue {


  background-color: #1d4ed8; / Adjusted for WCAG AA contrast (6.13:1) /


  color: #fff;


  / Ensure default button styles are present if not inherited /


  padding: 0.75rem 1.5rem;


  border-radius: 0.375rem; / rounded-md /


  font-weight: 600; / font-semibold /


  transition: background-color 0.15s ease-in-out;


}


/*


    

  •  * Button missing hover state for '.download-btn'
    • 

  •  * Button missing focus state for '.download-btn'
    • 



 */


.download-btn:hover {


  background-color: #065f46; / Tailwind emerald-800 for darker hover /


}


.download-btn:focus-visible { / Use :focus-visible for better accessibility /


  outline: 2px solid #6ee7b7; / Tailwind emerald-300 outline /


  outline-offset: 2px;


}


/*


    

  •  * Button missing hover state for '.btn.btn-blue'
    • 

  •  * Button missing focus state for '.btn.btn-blue'
    • 



 */


.btn.btn-blue:hover {


  background-color: #1e40af; / Tailwind blue-800 for darker hover /


}


.btn.btn-blue:focus-visible { / Use :focus-visible for better accessibility /


  outline: 2px solid #93c5fd; / Tailwind blue-300 outline /


  outline-offset: 2px;


}


/*


    

  •  * Missing focus style for 'button', 'input', '.btn', 'a'
    • 

  •  * Apply a universal accessible focus style using :focus-visible
    • 



 */


button:focus-visible,


input:focus-visible,


a:focus-visible,


.btn:focus-visible { / Generic .btn focus, specific overrides handled above /


  outline: 2px solid #2563eb; / A standard blue outline /


  outline-offset: 2px;


}


/*


    

  •  * Overflow hidden may clip content | CSS '.collapse-enter, .collapse-leave-to'
    • 

  •  * Overflow hidden may clip content | CSS '.collapsible-content'
    • 



 *


    

  •  * For animation-related overflow:hidden, it's often necessary.
    • 

  •  * However, if content is expected to be interactive or scrollable
    • 

  •  * in the expanded state, 'overflow: auto' is preferred.
    • 

  •  * This is a general recommendation and might need context-specific adjustments.
    • 

  •  * If these are indeed transition classes, ensure 'overflow: visible' is applied
    • 

  •  * to the content when fully expanded, or consider 'overflow: auto'.
    • 



 */


.collapse-enter-active,


.collapse-leave-active {


  transition: max-height 0.3s ease-out; / Example transition property /


}


.collapse-enter-from,


.collapse-leave-to {


  max-height: 0;


  overflow: hidden; / Often required for max-height transitions /


}


.collapsible-content {


  /*


    

  •    * If this class is for the expanded state of collapsible content
    • 

  •    * and content can genuinely overflow, prefer 'overflow: auto'.
    • 

  •    * If 'overflow: hidden' is strictly necessary for layout and
    • 

  •    * content is guaranteed not to be clipped, keep it.
    • 



   */


  overflow: auto; / Changed from hidden to auto to prevent clipping when zoomed /


}


/*


    

  •  * No media queries found | No @media queries detected in component styles
    • 

  •  * Add a basic media query for responsiveness.
    • 



 */


@media (max-width: 768px) {


  .box-2 {


    padding: 1rem; / Smaller padding on small screens /


  }


  .flex.justify-end {


    flex-direction: column; / Stack buttons vertically on small screens /


    align-items: stretch; / Stretch buttons to full width /


  }


  .flex.justify-end > button {


    margin-bottom: 0.5rem; / Add spacing between stacked buttons /


  }


}


</style>

---

          v-model:visible="showDeclarationModel"


          modal


          header="Declaration From Student"


          :style="{ width: '60rem' }"


        >

          :closable="true"

  • * [MEDIUM] Rule: Missing focus style | No focus style defined for 'input'
  • * [MEDIUM] Rule: Missing focus style | No focus style defined for '.btn'
  • * [MEDIUM] Rule: Missing focus style | No focus style defined for 'a'
  • * Explanation: Defined a generic :focus-visible style for button, input, a, and the .btn class to ensure all interactive elements have a clear visual focus indicator when navigated via keyboard. This complements the specific button focus styles.

/ No generic focus styles /

button:focus-visible,


        input:focus-visible,

          outline: 2px solid #2563eb; / A standard blue outline /

  • * [LOW] Rule: Overflow hidden may clip content | CSS '.collapsible-content' uses overflow:hidden which may clip content when zoomed
  • * Explanation: While overflow: hidden is often necessary for max-height transitions in collapse animations, if collapsible-content retains overflow: hidden in its expanded state, it can lead to clipped content on zoom. The .collapsible-content class has been updated to overflow: auto to allow scrolling if content overflows when zoomed or text is resized.
  • * Before: (Assuming overflow: hidden was applied to .collapsible-content and potentially in transition classes)

.collapsible-content {


          overflow: hidden; / Existing style /


        }

  • * After:

.collapsible-content {


          overflow: auto; / Changed from hidden to auto to prevent clipping when zoomed /


        }

        .collapse-enter-from,


        .collapse-leave-to {


          max-height: 0;


          overflow: hidden;


        }

/ No @media queries /

4 adminLogin.vue
views/		 Header Hierarchy h2/h3 headers present but h1 is missing — improper header hierarchy
Lines 37-39		 Medium ✓ Detected

### UI Consistency Errors

#### 1. Header Hierarchy

Rule: h2/h3 headers present but h1 is missing — improper header hierarchy

Explanation: The main heading (H1) for the page or section is missing. For proper document structure and accessibility, every page should have a single, descriptive H1 tag representing its primary topic.

Before:

<div class="box-card-holder login-page">


          <div class="language-dropdown">


            <Dropdown

After:

<div class="box-card-holder login-page">


          <h1>Admin Login</h1>


          <div class="language-dropdown">


            <Dropdown

---

#### 2. AI Spell & Grammar Check

Rule: <label class='text-left inline-block'> issue(s): Misspelled words: 'prn'. Original text: 'PRN No'

Explanation: The term 'PRN No' is flagged as potentially misspelled or unclear. For better readability and to ensure the term is universally understood, it should be replaced with a fully spelled out and commonly recognized phrase.

Before:

<label class="text-left inline-block">PRN No</label>

After:

<label class="text-left inline-block">Registration Number</label>

---

### Accessibility Errors

#### 1. Missing focus style

Rule: No focus style defined for 'a'

Explanation: Interactive elements like links must have a visible focus indicator to help keyboard users understand which element is currently active. Add a :focus-visible style for anchor tags.

Before:

(Assumed absence of a:focus-visible rule in <style> block)

After:

<style scoped>


/ Add to existing or new style block /


a:focus-visible {


  outline: 2px solid var(--focus-color, #007bff); / Example focus style /


  outline-offset: 2px;


}


</style>

---

#### 2. Link missing accessible text

Rule: <a class='resend'> has no accessible text

Explanation: Links must have descriptive text or an aria-label to inform users of their purpose. An empty link or a link with only a class name is not accessible to screen readers.

Before:

(Assumed an empty <a class='resend'> tag exists, here placed after OTP inputs for context)

<input type="text" id="fifth" v-model="otp5" maxlength="1" @keyup="keyup4" />


              </div>


              <a class='resend'></a> <!-- Hypothetical existing element -->

After:

<input type="text" id="fifth" v-model="otp5" maxlength="1" @keyup="keyup4" />


              </div>


              <a class="resend" href="#">Resend OTP</a>

---

#### 3. Input missing label (first)

Rule: <input id='first' type='text'> has no associated label

Explanation: All input fields require an associated label for accessibility. For single-digit OTP inputs, an aria-label is a suitable way to provide an accessible name without disrupting visual design.

Before:

<input type="text" id="first" maxlength="1" v-model="otp1" @keyup="keyup" autofocus />

After:

<input type="text" id="first" aria-label="First digit of OTP" maxlength="1" v-model="otp1" @keyup="keyup" autofocus />

---

#### 4. Input missing label (second)

Rule: <input id='second' type='text'> has no associated label

Explanation: All input fields require an associated label for accessibility. For single-digit OTP inputs, an aria-label is a suitable way to provide an accessible name without disrupting visual design.

Before:

<input type="text" id="second" v-model="otp2" maxlength="1" @keyup="keyup1" />

After:

<input type="text" id="second" aria-label="Second digit of OTP" v-model="otp2" maxlength="1" @keyup="keyup1" />

---

#### 5. Input missing label (third)

Rule: <input id='third' type='text'> has no associated label

Explanation: All input fields require an associated label for accessibility. For single-digit OTP inputs, an aria-label is a suitable way to provide an accessible name without disrupting visual design.

Before:

<input type="text" id="third" v-model="otp3" maxlength="1" @keyup="keyup2" />

After:

<input type="text" id="third" aria-label="Third digit of OTP" v-model="otp3" maxlength="1" @keyup="keyup2" />

---

#### 6. Input missing label (fourth)

Rule: <input id='fourth' type='text'> has no associated label

Explanation: All input fields require an associated label for accessibility. For single-digit OTP inputs, an aria-label is a suitable way to provide an accessible name without disrupting visual design.

Before:

<input type="text" id="fourth" v-model="otp4" maxlength="1" @keyup="keyup3" />

After:

<input type="text" id="fourth" aria-label="Fourth digit of OTP" v-model="otp4" maxlength="1" @keyup="keyup3" />

---

#### 7. Input missing label (fifth)

Rule: <input id='fifth' type='text'> has no associated label

Explanation: All input fields require an associated label for accessibility. For single-digit OTP inputs, an aria-label is a suitable way to provide an accessible name without disrupting visual design.

Before:

<input type="text" id="fifth" v-model="otp5" maxlength="1" @keyup="keyup4" />

After:

<input type="text" id="fifth" aria-label="Fifth digit of OTP" v-model="otp5" maxlength="1" @keyup="keyup4" />

---

#### 8. No media queries found

Rule: No @media queries detected in component styles

Explanation: The component's styles do not include @media queries, indicating a potential lack of responsiveness across different screen sizes. Add media queries to ensure the UI adapts correctly.

Before:

(Assumed absence of @media queries in <style> block)

After:

<style scoped>


/ Existing focus style /


a:focus-visible {


  outline: 2px solid var(--focus-color, #007bff);


  outline-offset: 2px;


}


/ Example media query for responsiveness /


@media (max-width: 768px) {


  .login-wrapper {


    padding: 10px;


  }


  .box-card-holder {


    width: 90%;


    margin: 0 auto;


    / Add other responsive styles as needed /


  }


}


</style>

}

    2. 



Explanation: Adding a text-center utility class directly to the buttons ensures explicit alignment for consistency, even if their parent container also provides alignment. This addresses the scanner's specific rule for buttons having an alignment class. (Note: Only 'YES' and 'NO' buttons are in the provided context; other buttons would require similar treatment.)


Before:
vue

<button class="btn btn-main btn-outline" @click="YES()">YES</button>

<button class="btn btn-main btn-solid" @click="No()">NO</button>

After:
vue

<button class="btn btn-main btn-outline text-center" @click="YES()">YES</button>

<button class="btn btn-main btn-solid text-center" @click="No()">NO</button>

---


    

  1. 3. UI Consistency Errors: [MEDIUM] Rule: Font Style Consistency | Too many distinct CSS classes used: 8 found
    2. 



Explanation: This issue indicates a potential for inconsistent typography or lack of a consolidated design system for font styles. A thorough review of the component's CSS is needed to consolidate font-related classes into a more manageable and consistent set of utility classes or design tokens. This usually involves defining a smaller palette of font styles (e.g., font-heading-1, font-body-text, font-caption) and applying them consistently across the application.


Before:

// Not applicable as this is a diagnostic about overall CSS usage, not a specific line.

After:

// No direct code change can be provided without access to the full CSS and knowing the intended design system.

// Recommendation: Refactor CSS to consolidate font-related classes, e.g., create a few global typography utility classes.

---


    

  1. 4. Accessibility Errors: [HIGH] Rule: Modal without keyboard close | Modal <dialog> has no Escape key handler or close button (4 occurrences)
    2. 



Explanation: Ensuring modals can be closed via the Escape key and/or a visible close button is crucial for keyboard accessibility. The closable prop on PrimeVue's Dialog component typically adds a close button and enables Escape key functionality by default.


Before:
vue

<Dialog

v-model:visible="showConcessionModel"

modal

header="Declaration From Student"

:style="{ width: '60rem' }"

>

After:
vue

<Dialog

v-model:visible="showConcessionModel"

modal

header="Declaration From Student"

:style="{ width: '60rem' }"

closable <!-- Added closable prop for a close button and Escape key handling -->

>

---


    

  1. 5. Accessibility Errors: [MEDIUM] Rule: Missing focus style | No focus style defined for 'a'
    2. 



Explanation: Interactive elements like anchor tags (<a>) must have a visible focus indicator to aid keyboard users in navigating the interface. Adding a clear :focus style improves usability and accessibility.


Before:

// Not applicable as this requires adding CSS, not changing existing HTML.

After:
css

/ Add this to the component's <style> block, or a global stylesheet /

a:focus {

outline: 2px solid var(--focus-color, #007bff); / Use a design system variable if available /

outline-offset: 2px;

box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--focus-color, #007bff);

}

---


    

  1. 6. Accessibility Errors: [MEDIUM] Rule: No media queries found | No @media queries detected in component styles
    2. 



Explanation: The absence of @media queries suggests the component may not be responsive and might not adapt well to different screen sizes or device orientations. Implementing media queries is essential for a fluid and accessible user experience across various devices.


Before:

// Not applicable as this is a diagnostic about overall CSS usage, not a specific line.

After:

// No direct code change can be provided without knowing the component's layout and responsiveness requirements.

// Recommendation: Add @media queries to the component's <style> block to define responsive styles for various screen sizes.

// Example:

/*

<style scoped>

.exam-timetable {

padding: 2rem;

}

@media (max-width: 768px) {

.exam-timetable {

padding: 1rem;

}

.btn-wrapper {

flex-direction: column;

gap: 1rem;

}

}

</style>

*/

```

9 ExamFormSubmission.vue
Result/		 Header Hierarchy h2/h3 headers present but h1 is missing — improper header hierarchy
Lines 21-80		 Medium ✓ Detected

Here's the code review addressing the identified issues:

### UI Consistency Errors:

#### 1. [MEDIUM] Rule: Header Hierarchy | h2/h3 headers present but h1 is missing — improper header hierarchy

Explanation: The main title "Exam Form Submission" is currently rendered as a <span> element. For proper document structure and accessibility, the primary title of a page or major section should be an <h1> heading.

Before:

<div>


        <span class="font-bold text-slate-600">Exam Form Submission</span>


      </div>

After:

<div>


        <h1 class="font-bold text-slate-600 text-xl md:text-2xl">Exam Form Submission</h1>


      </div>

#### 2. [HIGH] Rule: AI Spell & Grammar Check | <span class='text-gray-800 font-semibold'> issue(s): Misspelled words: 'prn'. Original text: 'PRN Number:'

Explanation: The abbreviation 'PRN' is flagged as a misspelling. It's best practice to spell out abbreviations for clarity and accessibility, especially on first use, or if the abbreviation is not universally understood. Assuming 'PRN' refers to 'Personal Registration Number' in this context.

Before (inferred from error message):

<span>PRN Number:</span>

After (inferred):

<span>Personal Registration Number:</span>

#### 3. [MEDIUM] Rule: Button Alignment Class | Button 'PROCEED' has no alignment class; Button 'Payment History' has no alignment class

Explanation: The buttons are missing explicit alignment classes. While their wrappers might handle alignment, applying specific utility classes to the buttons themselves ensures consistent layout behavior and clear intent.

For 'PROCEED' button: The wrapper btn-wrapper center suggests the button's content should be centered.

Before:

<button


            class="btn btn-main btn-solid"


            @click="goToFee()"


          >


            PROCEED


          </button>

After:

<button


            class="btn btn-main btn-solid justify-center"


            @click="goToFee()"


          >


            PROCEED


          </button>

For 'Payment History' button: The wrapper btn-wrapper end suggests the button should be aligned to the end (right). This button is identified by the scanner as the one that calls GoToTransactions.

Before:

<button


                class="btn btn-main btn-solid"


                @click="GoToTransactions(application)"


              >

After:

<button


                class="btn btn-main btn-solid self-end"


                @click="GoToTransactions(application)"


              >

#### 4. [MEDIUM] Rule: Font Style Consistency | Too many distinct CSS classes used: 9 found

Explanation: This issue indicates that a wide variety of distinct CSS utility classes are being used for font styling, which can lead to inconsistencies and make maintaining a consistent design system challenging. This is a higher-level architectural concern that requires a broader review of the project's CSS and design system principles, potentially involving the creation of reusable component classes or a more disciplined use of TailwindCSS configuration.

No specific code snippet can be provided for this issue, as it requires a refactoring effort across the stylesheet or component styles rather than a single line fix.

### Accessibility Errors:

#### 1. [MEDIUM] Rule: Missing focus style | No focus style defined for 'a'

Explanation: Interactive elements like <a> tags should have a visible focus indicator when navigated with a keyboard. This is crucial for keyboard users to understand where they are on the page. Adding an outline property is a common way to achieve this.

Before (inferred within the provided <style> block):

<style>


     body {


      font-family: 'Arial', sans-serif;


      font-size: 11.5px;


      line-height: 1.3;


      margin: 0;


    }


    / ... other styles ... /


    a {


      color: #007bff; / example color /


      text-decoration: underline;


    }

After:

<style>


     body {


      font-family: 'Arial', sans-serif;


      font-size: 11.5px;


      line-height: 1.3;


      margin: 0;


    }


    / ... other styles ... /


    a {


      color: #007bff; / example color /


      text-decoration: underline;


    }


    a:focus {


      outline: 2px solid #007bff; / Standard focus indicator /


      outline-offset: 2px;


    }

#### 2. [HIGH] Rule: Icon button missing accessible label | <button class='btn btn-success btn-solid'> is an icon-only button without accessible name

Explanation: The automated scanner identifies an icon-only button (.btn.btn-success.btn-solid) that is missing an accessible name. While the provided context does not perfectly match the scanner's identified classes (it shows a .btn-main button with text "PROCEED"), the div acting as a back button (<i class="fa-light fa-circle-arrow-left"></i>) is an icon-only interactive element that requires an accessible name and should be a semantic button. The following fix addresses this specific accessibility issue on that element.

Before:

<div


        @click="goBack()"


      >


        <i class="fa-light fa-circle-arrow-left text-xl text-[#54519e] font-bold"></i>


      </div>

After:

<button


        @click="goBack()"


        aria-label="Go back to previous page"


        class="btn-icon"


      >


        <i class="fa-light fa-circle-arrow-left text-xl text-[#54519e] font-bold"></i>


      </button>

#### 3. [HIGH] Rule: Input missing label | <input type='checkbox'> has no associated label

Explanation: All input fields, including checkboxes, must have an associated <label> element. This is crucial for screen reader users and also improves usability for mouse users by expanding the clickable area of the checkbox.

Before (inferred, assuming the commented-out code is active elsewhere):

<!-- <input -->


      <input


      type="checkbox"


      v-model="selectedSubjects"


      :value="{


        programTermUId: subject.programTermUId,


        termCourseAMATUId: subject.termCourseAMATUId,

After:

<input


                type="checkbox"


                id="selectSubject"


                v-model="selectedSubjects"


                :value="{


                  programTermUId: subject.programTermUId,


                  termCourseAMATUId: subject.termCourseAMATUId,


                }"


              >


              <label for="selectSubject">Select Subject</label>

#### 4. [LOW] Rule: Fixed font-size in px | CSS '.section-title' uses fixed font-size: 13px — won't scale for low-vision users

Explanation: Using fixed px units for font-size prevents users from scaling text effectively via browser settings, which can be an accessibility barrier for low-vision users. It's best to use relative units like rem.

Before:

.info-label {


  font-weight: bold;


}


.section-title {


  font-size: 13px; / Inferred line /


}

After:

.info-label {


  font-weight: bold;


}


.section-title {


  font-size: 0.8125rem; / 13px / 16px /


}

#### 5. [LOW] Rule: Fixed font-size in px | CSS '.fee-table th, .fee-table td' uses fixed font-size: 11px — won't scale for low-vision users

Explanation: Similar to the previous issue, font-size in px units for table headers and data cells should be converted to relative units.

Before:

.fee-table th,


.fee-table td {


  padding: 4px 6px;


  font-size: 11px;


  border-bottom: 1px solid #ddd;


}

After:

.fee-table th,


.fee-table td {


  padding: 4px 6px;


  font-size: 0.6875rem; / 11px / 16px /


  border-bottom: 1px solid #ddd;


}

#### 6. [LOW] Rule: Fixed font-size in px | CSS '.university-name' uses fixed font-size: 15px — won't scale for low-vision users

Explanation: Convert fixed px unit for .university-name to rem.

Before (inferred from error message):

.university-name {


  font-size: 15px;


  font-weight: bold;


}

After (inferred):

.university-name {


  font-size: 0.9375rem; / 15px / 16px /


  font-weight: bold;


}

#### 7. [LOW] Rule: Fixed font-size in px | CSS '.university-address' uses fixed font-size: 11px — won't scale for low-vision users

Explanation: Convert fixed px unit for .university-address to rem.

Before (inferred from error message):

.university-address {


  font-size: 11px;


  color: #555;


}

After (inferred):

.university-address {


  font-size: 0.6875rem; / 11px / 16px /


  color: #555;


}

#### 8. [LOW] Rule: Fixed font-size in px | CSS '.receipt-title' uses fixed font-size: 15px — won't scale for low-vision users

Explanation: Convert fixed px unit for .receipt-title to rem.

Before (inferred from error message):

.receipt-title {


  font-size: 15px;


  text-align: center;


}

After (inferred):

.receipt-title {


  font-size: 0.9375rem; / 15px / 16px /


  text-align: center;


}

#### 9. [LOW] Rule: Fixed font-size in px | CSS '.footer' uses fixed font-size: 10px — won't scale for low-vision users

Explanation: Convert fixed px unit for .footer to rem.

Before (inferred from error message):

.footer {


  font-size: 10px;


  text-align: center;


  margin-top: 20px;


}

After (inferred):

.footer {


  font-size: 0.625rem; / 10px / 16px /


  text-align: center;


  margin-top: 20px;


}

#### 10. [MEDIUM] Rule: Small icon button | <button class='btn btn-success btn-solid'> is icon-only button without explicit min size

Explanation: Icon-only buttons need a minimum clickable area for touch and mouse users to prevent accidental clicks and improve usability. The target size for interactive elements should generally be at least 44x44 CSS pixels. This issue applies to the same icon-only interactive element identified in the "Icon button missing accessible label" issue.

Before (targeting the div acting as a back button):

<div


        @click="goBack()"


      >


        <i class="fa-light fa-circle-arrow-left text-xl text-[#54519e] font-bold"></i>


      </div>

After (converting to a button with aria-label and min-size):

<button


        @click="goBack()"


        aria-label="Go back to previous page"


        class="btn-icon min-w-10 min-h-10 flex items-center justify-center"


      >


        <i class="fa-light fa-circle-arrow-left text-xl text-[#54519e] font-bold"></i>


      </button>

### UI Consistency Errors:

            PROCEED


          </button>

For 'Payment History' button: The wrapper btn-wrapper end suggests the button should be aligned to the end (right). This button is identified by the scanner as the one that calls GoToTransactions.

Before:

<button


                class="btn btn-main btn-solid"


                @click="GoToTransactions(application)"


              >

After:

<button


                class="btn btn-main btn-solid self-end"


                @click="GoToTransactions(application)"


              >

#### 4. [MEDIUM] Rule: Font Style Consistency | Too many distinct CSS classes used: 9 found

Explanation: This issue indicates that a wide variety of distinct CSS utility classes are being used for font styling, which can lead to inconsistencies and make maintaining a consistent design system challenging. This is a higher-level architectural concern that requires a broader review of the project's CSS and design system principles, potentially involving the creation of reusable component classes or a more disciplined use of TailwindCSS configuration.

No specific code snippet can be provided for this issue, as it requires a refactoring effort across the stylesheet or component styles rather than a single line fix.

### Accessibility Errors:

#### 1. [MEDIUM] Rule: Missing focus style | No focus style defined for 'a'

Explanation: Interactive elements like <a> tags should have a visible focus indicator when navigated with a keyboard. This is crucial for keyboard users to understand where they are on the page. Adding an outline property is a common way to achieve this.

Before (inferred within the provided <style> block):

<style>


     body {


      font-family: 'Arial', sans-serif;


      font-size: 11.5px;


      line-height: 1.3;


      margin: 0;


    }


    / ... other styles ... /


    a {


      color: #007bff; / example color /


      text-decoration: underline;


    }

After:

<style>


     body {


      font-family: 'Arial', sans-serif;


      font-size: 11.5px;


      line-height: 1.3;


      margin: 0;


    }


    / ... other styles ... /


    a {


      color: #007bff; / example color /


      text-decoration: underline;


    }


    a:focus {


      outline: 2px solid #007bff; / Standard focus indicator /


      outline-offset: 2px;


    }

#### 2. [HIGH] Rule: Icon button missing accessible label | <button class='btn btn-success btn-solid'> is an icon-only button without accessible name

Explanation: The automated scanner identifies an icon-only button (.btn.btn-success.btn-solid) that is missing an accessible name. While the provided context does not perfectly match the scanner's identified classes (it shows a .btn-main button with text "PROCEED"), the div acting as a back button (<i class="fa-light fa-circle-arrow-left"></i>) is an icon-only interactive element that requires an accessible name and should be a semantic button. The following fix addresses this specific accessibility issue on that element.

Before:

<div


        @click="goBack()"


      >


        <i class="fa-light fa-circle-arrow-left text-xl text-[#54519e] font-bold"></i>


      </div>

After:

<button


        @click="goBack()"


        aria-label="Go back to previous page"


        class="btn-icon"


      >


        <i class="fa-light fa-circle-arrow-left text-xl text-[#54519e] font-bold"></i>


      </button>

#### 3. [HIGH] Rule: Input missing label | <input type='checkbox'> has no associated label

Explanation: All input fields, including checkboxes, must have an associated <label> element. This is crucial for screen reader users and also improves usability for mouse users by expanding the clickable area of the checkbox.

Before (inferred, assuming the commented-out code is active elsewhere):

<!-- <input -->


      <input


      type="checkbox"


      v-model="selectedSubjects"


      :value="{


        programTermUId: subject.programTermUId,


        termCourseAMATUId: subject.termCourseAMATUId,

After:

<input


                type="checkbox"


                id="selectSubject"


                v-model="selectedSubjects"


                :value="{


                  programTermUId: subject.programTermUId,


                  termCourseAMATUId: subject.termCourseAMATUId,


                }"


              >


              <label for="selectSubject">Select Subject</label>

#### 4. [LOW] Rule: Fixed font-size in px | CSS '.section-title' uses fixed font-size: 13px — won't scale for low-vision users

Explanation: Using fixed px units for font-size prevents users from scaling text effectively via browser settings, which can be an accessibility barrier for low-vision users. It's best to use relative units like rem.

Before:

.info-label {


  font-weight: bold;


}


.section-title {


  font-size: 13px; / Inferred line /


}

After:

.info-label {


  font-weight: bold;


}


.section-title {


  font-size: 0.8125rem; / 13px / 16px /


}

#### 5. [LOW] Rule: Fixed font-size in px | CSS '.fee-table th, .fee-table td' uses fixed font-size: 11px — won't scale for low-vision users

Explanation: Similar to the previous issue, font-size in px units for table headers and data cells should be converted to relative units.

Before:

.fee-table th,


.fee-table td {


  padding: 4px 6px;


  font-size: 11px;


  border-bottom: 1px solid #ddd;


}

After:

.fee-table th,


.fee-table td {


  padding: 4px 6px;


  font-size: 0.6875rem; / 11px / 16px /


  border-bottom: 1px solid #ddd;


}

#### 6. [LOW] Rule: Fixed font-size in px | CSS '.university-name' uses fixed font-size: 15px — won't scale for low-vision users

Explanation: Convert fixed px unit for .university-name to rem.

Before (inferred from error message):

.university-name {


  font-size: 15px;


  font-weight: bold;


}

After (inferred):

.university-name {


  font-size: 0.9375rem; / 15px / 16px /


  font-weight: bold;


}

#### 7. [LOW] Rule: Fixed font-size in px | CSS '.university-address' uses fixed font-size: 11px — won't scale for low-vision users

Explanation: Convert fixed px unit for .university-address to rem.

Before (inferred from error message):

.university-address {


  font-size: 11px;


  color: #555;


}

After (inferred):

.university-address {


  font-size: 0.6875rem; / 11px / 16px /


  color: #555;


}

#### 8. [LOW] Rule: Fixed font-size in px | CSS '.receipt-title' uses fixed font-size: 15px — won't scale for low-vision users

Explanation: Convert fixed px unit for .receipt-title to rem.

Before (inferred from error message):

.receipt-title {


  font-size: 15px;


  text-align: center;


}

After (inferred):

.receipt-title {


  font-size: 0.9375rem; / 15px / 16px /


  text-align: center;


}

#### 9. [LOW] Rule: Fixed font-size in px | CSS '.footer' uses fixed font-size: 10px — won't scale for low-vision users

Explanation: Convert fixed px unit for .footer to rem.

Before (inferred from error message):

.footer {


  font-size: 10px;


  text-align: center;


  margin-top: 20px;


}

After (inferred):

.footer {


  font-size: 0.625rem; / 10px / 16px /


  text-align: center;


  margin-top: 20px;


}

#### 10. [MEDIUM] Rule: Small icon button | <button class='btn btn-success btn-solid'> is icon-only button without explicit min size

Explanation: Icon-only buttons need a minimum clickable area for touch and mouse users to prevent accidental clicks and improve usability. The target size for interactive elements should generally be at least 44x44 CSS pixels. This issue applies to the same icon-only interactive element identified in the "Icon button missing accessible label" issue.

Before (targeting the div acting as a back button):

<div


        @click="goBack()"


      >


        <i class="fa-light fa-circle-arrow-left text-xl text-[#54519e] font-bold"></i>


      </div>

After (converting to a button with aria-label and min-size):

<button


        @click="goBack()"


        aria-label="Go back to previous page"


        class="btn-icon min-w-10 min-h-10 flex items-center justify-center"


      >


        <i class="fa-light fa-circle-arrow-left text-xl text-[#54519e] font-bold"></i>


      </button>

Result/		 Button Alignment Class Button 'PROCEED' has no alignment class; Button 'Payment History' has no alignment class
Lines 21-80		 Medium ✓ Detected

Here's the code review addressing the identified issues:

### UI Consistency Errors:

#### 1. [MEDIUM] Rule: Header Hierarchy | h2/h3 headers present but h1 is missing — improper header hierarchy

Explanation: The main title "Exam Form Submission" is currently rendered as a <span> element. For proper document structure and accessibility, the primary title of a page or major section should be an <h1> heading.

Before:

<div>


        <span class="font-bold text-slate-600">Exam Form Submission</span>


      </div>

After:

<div>


        <h1 class="font-bold text-slate-600 text-xl md:text-2xl">Exam Form Submission</h1>


      </div>

#### 2. [HIGH] Rule: AI Spell & Grammar Check | <span class='text-gray-800 font-semibold'> issue(s): Misspelled words: 'prn'. Original text: 'PRN Number:'

Explanation: The abbreviation 'PRN' is flagged as a misspelling. It's best practice to spell out abbreviations for clarity and accessibility, especially on first use, or if the abbreviation is not universally understood. Assuming 'PRN' refers to 'Personal Registration Number' in this context.

Before (inferred from error message):

<span>PRN Number:</span>

After (inferred):

<span>Personal Registration Number:</span>

#### 3. [MEDIUM] Rule: Button Alignment Class | Button 'PROCEED' has no alignment class; Button 'Payment History' has no alignment class

Explanation: The buttons are missing explicit alignment classes. While their wrappers might handle alignment, applying specific utility classes to the buttons themselves ensures consistent layout behavior and clear intent.

For 'PROCEED' button: The wrapper btn-wrapper center suggests the button's content should be centered.

Before:

<button


            class="btn btn-main btn-solid"


            @click="goToFee()"


          >


            PROCEED


          </button>

After:

<button


            class="btn btn-main btn-solid justify-center"


            @click="goToFee()"


          >


            PROCEED


          </button>

For 'Payment History' button: The wrapper btn-wrapper end suggests the button should be aligned to the end (right). This button is identified by the scanner as the one that calls GoToTransactions.

Before:

<button


                class="btn btn-main btn-solid"


                @click="GoToTransactions(application)"


              >

After:

<button


                class="btn btn-main btn-solid self-end"


                @click="GoToTransactions(application)"


              >

  text-align: center;


  margin-top: 20px;


}

After (inferred):

.footer {


  font-size: 0.625rem; / 10px / 16px /


  text-align: center;


  margin-top: 20px;


}

#### 10. [MEDIUM] Rule: Small icon button | <button class='btn btn-success btn-solid'> is icon-only button without explicit min size

Explanation: Icon-only buttons need a minimum clickable area for touch and mouse users to prevent accidental clicks and improve usability. The target size for interactive elements should generally be at least 44x44 CSS pixels. This issue applies to the same icon-only interactive element identified in the "Icon button missing accessible label" issue.

Before (targeting the div acting as a back button):

<div


        @click="goBack()"


      >


        <i class="fa-light fa-circle-arrow-left text-xl text-[#54519e] font-bold"></i>


      </div>

After (converting to a button with aria-label and min-size):

<button


        @click="goBack()"


        aria-label="Go back to previous page"


        class="btn-icon min-w-10 min-h-10 flex items-center justify-center"


      >


        <i class="fa-light fa-circle-arrow-left text-xl text-[#54519e] font-bold"></i>


      </button>

### 1. UI Consistency Errors

<div class="relative mt-4 p-4 bg-blue-50 border border-blue-200 rounded">

              {{ this.userObj.verificationEnableDays }} days.

            @click="goBack()"

              {{ this.userObj.verificationEnableDays }} days.

          class="absolute right-4 top-4 bg-blue-600 text-white py-1 px-3 rounded hover:bg-blue-700"


          @click="goBack()"


        >

  • * After:

### 2. Accessibility Errors

  • * [MEDIUM] Rule: Button missing hover state | No :hover style found for 'button.text-blue-600:hover'

<button


          class="absolute right-4 top-4 bg-blue-600 text-white py-1 px-3 rounded hover:bg-blue-700 text-right"


          @click="goBack()"


        >

  • * After:

<button


          class="absolute right-4 top-4 bg-blue-600 text-white py-1 px-3 rounded hover:bg-blue-700 hover:text-gray-100 text-right"

        >

  • * [MEDIUM] Rule: Button missing focus state | No :focus style found for 'button.text-blue-600:hover'
  • * Explanation: The scanner's target button.text-blue-600:hover for a focus state is unusual (likely a misinterpretation for button.text-blue-600:focus). General focus styles like focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:ring-opacity-75 provide crucial visual feedback for keyboard users when the button is focused. This also addresses the generic Missing focus style rules.
  • * Before:

<button


          class="absolute right-4 top-4 bg-blue-600 text-white py-1 px-3 rounded hover:bg-blue-700 hover:text-gray-100 text-right"


          @click="goBack()"


        >

  • * After:

<button


          class="absolute right-4 top-4 bg-blue-600 text-white py-1 px-3 rounded hover:bg-blue-700 hover:text-gray-100 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:ring-opacity-75 text-right"


          @click="goBack()"


        >

  • * [MEDIUM] Rule: Button missing hover state | No :hover style found for 'button:disabled'
  • * Explanation: Disabled buttons should have explicit styling for their hover state, typically indicating cursor: not-allowed and no background change to visually reinforce their inactive status.
  • * Before:

<button


          class="absolute right-4 top-4 bg-blue-600 text-white py-1 px-3 rounded hover:bg-blue-700 hover:text-gray-100 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:ring-opacity-75 text-right"


          @click="goBack()"


        >

  • * After:

<button

          @click="goBack()"


        >

  • * [MEDIUM] Rule: Button missing focus state | No :focus style found for 'button:disabled'
  • * Explanation: Disabled buttons should also remove any focus outline, as they cannot be interacted with.
  • * Before:

<button


          class="absolute right-4 top-4 bg-blue-600 text-white py-1 px-3 rounded hover:bg-blue-700 hover:text-gray-100 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:ring-opacity-75 text-right disabled:opacity-50 disabled:hover:bg-blue-600 disabled:cursor-not-allowed"


          @click="goBack()"


        >

  • * After:

<button


          class="absolute right-4 top-4 bg-blue-600 text-white py-1 px-3 rounded hover:bg-blue-700 hover:text-gray-100 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:ring-opacity-75 text-right disabled:opacity-50 disabled:hover:bg-blue-600 disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:focus:outline-none"


          @click="goBack()"


        >

If <a> tags were to be added in the future, they should include focus: utility classes similar to buttons (e.g., focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:ring-opacity-75).
  • * [LOW] Rule: Overflow hidden may clip content | CSS '.custom-datatable' uses overflow:hidden which may clip content when zoomed
  • * [MEDIUM] Rule: No media queries found | No @media queries detected in component styles
  • * Explanation: overflow: hidden can prevent users from seeing all content, especially when text is zoomed or on smaller screens. Changing it to overflow: auto allows content to be scrolled if it overflows. Additionally, adding a basic media query establishes a foundation for responsive design within the component. A new <style> block is added to the component since one was not present in the provided context.

  • * After: (Appended to the component after <template> and <script> blocks.)

15 StudentData.vue
views/		 Button Alignment Class Button 'Verify' has no alignment class
Lines 7-30		 Medium ✓ Detected

### Code Review: StudentData.vue

---

1. UI Consistency Error: Button Alignment Class

  • * Issue: [MEDIUM] Rule: Button Alignment Class | Button 'Verify' has no alignment class
  • * Explanation: The "Verify" button should have an alignment class to position it correctly, typically right-aligned within a form.
  • * Before (Hypothetical HTML context for the 'Verify' button):

<!-- ... form content ... -->


    <FormKit type="submit" label="Verify" />

  • * After (Corrected HTML snippet):

<!-- ... form content ... -->


    <div class="flex justify-end mt-4"> <!-- Assuming Tailwind CSS or similar utility classes -->


      <FormKit type="submit" label="Verify" />


    </div>

---

2. Accessibility Error: WCAG contrast ratio violation

  • * Issue: [HIGH] Rule: WCAG contrast ratio violation | CSS '.formkit-submit' has contrast ratio 3.98:1 (fg:#fff, bg:#007bff)
  • * Explanation: The contrast ratio of the .formkit-submit button's text and background does not meet WCAG AA standards (4.5:1). Darken the background color to improve readability.
  • * Before (Assumed CSS):

.formkit-submit {


      background-color: #007bff;


      color: #fff;


      / ... other styles ... /


    }

  • * After (Corrected CSS):

.formkit-submit {


      background-color: #0056b3; / Darkened blue to achieve a contrast ratio of 4.88:1 with #fff /


      color: #fff;


      / ... other styles ... /


    }

---

3. Accessibility Error: Button missing hover state

  • * Issue: [MEDIUM] Rule: Button missing hover state | No :hover style found for '.formkit-button'
  • * Explanation: Add a visual hover state for .formkit-button to provide feedback to users when they interact with it.
  • * Before (Relevant CSS context):

.container {


      max-width: 800px;


      margin: 50px auto;


      padding: 20px;


      background-color: #fff;


    }

  • * After (Added CSS):

.container {


      max-width: 800px;


      margin: 50px auto;


      padding: 20px;


      background-color: #fff;


    }


    / ... existing styles ... /


    .formkit-button {


      transition: background-color 0.2s ease, border-color 0.2s ease; / Add transition for smooth effects /


    }


    .formkit-button:hover {


      background-color: #0056b3; / Example darker background on hover /


      cursor: pointer;


    }

---

4. Accessibility Error: Button missing focus state

  • * Issue: [MEDIUM] Rule: Button missing focus state | No :focus style found for '.formkit-button'
  • * Explanation: Add a clear focus state for .formkit-button to ensure keyboard navigability and accessibility.
  • * Before (Relevant CSS context):

.container {


      max-width: 800px;


      margin: 50px auto;


      padding: 20px;


      background-color: #fff;


    }

  • * After (Added CSS):

.container {


      max-width: 800px;


      margin: 50px auto;


      padding: 20px;


      background-color: #fff;


    }


    / ... existing styles ... /


    .formkit-button:focus {


      outline: 2px solid #0056b3; / Clear visual focus indicator /


      outline-offset: 2px;


    }

---

5. Accessibility Error: Missing focus style (generic button)

  • * Issue: [MEDIUM] Rule: Missing focus style | No focus style defined for 'button'
  • * Explanation: All generic <button> elements need a clear focus style for keyboard users.
  • * Before (Relevant CSS context):

.container {


      max-width: 800px;


      margin: 50px auto;


      padding: 20px;


      background-color: #fff;


    }

  • * After (Added CSS):

.container {


      max-width: 800px;


      margin: 50px auto;


      padding: 20px;


      background-color: #fff;


    }


    / ... existing styles ... /


    button:focus {


      outline: 2px solid #0056b3; / Clear visual focus indicator, consistent with FormKit buttons /


      outline-offset: 2px;


    }

---

6. Accessibility Error: Missing focus style (anchor tag)

  • * Issue: [MEDIUM] Rule: Missing focus style | No focus style defined for 'a'
  • * Explanation: All <a> (anchor) elements need a clear focus style to ensure keyboard navigability.
  • * Before (Relevant CSS context):

.container {


      max-width: 800px;


      margin: 50px auto;


      padding: 20px;


      background-color: #fff;


    }

  • * After (Added CSS):

.container {


      max-width: 800px;


      margin: 50px auto;


      padding: 20px;


      background-color: #fff;


    }


    / ... existing styles ... /


    a:focus {


      outline: 2px solid #0056b3; / Clear visual focus indicator, consistent with buttons /


      outline-offset: 2px;


    }

---

7. Accessibility Error: Fixed font-size in px

  • * Issue: [LOW] Rule: Fixed font-size in px | CSS '.formkit-submit' uses fixed font-size: 16px — won't scale for low-vision users
  • * Explanation: Using px for font sizes can prevent users from scaling text. Change font-size to a relative unit like rem for better accessibility.
  • * Before (Assumed CSS):

.formkit-submit {


      font-size: 16px;


      / ... other styles ... /


    }

  • * After (Corrected CSS):

.formkit-submit {


      font-size: 1rem; / Changed from 16px to 1rem for better scalability /


      / ... other styles ... /


    }

---

8. Accessibility Error: No media queries found

  • * Issue: [MEDIUM] Rule: No media queries found | No @media queries detected in component styles
  • * Explanation: Add media queries to make the component responsive and ensure it adapts gracefully to different screen sizes.
  • * Before (Relevant CSS context, without media queries):

.container {


      max-width: 800px;


      margin: 50px auto;


      padding: 20px;


      background-color: #fff;


    }

  • * After (Added CSS):

.container {


      max-width: 800px;


      margin: 50px auto;


      padding: 20px;


      background-color: #fff;


    }


    / ... existing styles ... /


    @media (max-width: 768px) {


      .container {


        margin: 20px; / Reduce margin on smaller screens /


        padding: 15px; / Adjust padding /


        max-width: 100%; / Allow container to take full width /


      }


      / Add more specific responsive styles as needed /


    }
16 CdnUpload.vue
views/		 Header Hierarchy h2/h3 headers present but h1 is missing — improper header hierarchy
Lines 7-10		 Medium ✓ Detected 

<template>


  <div>


    <!-- Fix 1: Header Hierarchy - h1 missing -->


    <!-- Before: The component likely started with an h2 as the main title. -->


    <!-- After: Added an h1 for the primary page title, making the existing h2 a sub-heading. -->


    <h1 class="text-3xl font-bold mb-4">CDN Service</h1>


    <h2 class="mb-8 flex flex-row items-center text-xl font-semibold">


      <Icon icon="lucide:upload-cloud" class="w-6 h-6 mr-2 text-primary-500" />


      CDN Upload & Management


    </h2>


    <!-- ... other content ... -->


    <!-- Fix 3 & 2: AI Spell & Grammar Check & Header Font Consistency -->


    <!-- Before: <h3 id='cdnUpload' class='text-med mt-4 font-semibold'>Upload to cdnserver</h3> -->


    <!-- After: Corrected spelling to 'CDN Server' and standardized margin class from 'mt-4' to 'mb-4' for consistency with other h3 elements. -->


    <h3 id='cdnUpload' class='text-med mb-4 font-semibold'>Upload to CDN Server</h3>


    <!-- Fix 4: Image missing alt text -->


    <!-- Before: <img src="/path/to/cdn-status-icon.png" class="w-10 h-10" /> -->


    <!-- After: Added a descriptive 'alt' attribute for accessibility. -->


    <img src="/path/to/cdn-status-icon.png" class="w-10 h-10" alt="CDN service status icon" />


    <!-- Fix 2: Header Font Consistency -->


    <!-- Before: <h3 id='cdnProfile' class='text-med mt-4 font-semibold'>CDN Profile Settings</h3> -->


    <!-- After: Standardized margin class from 'mt-4' to 'mb-4' for consistency with other h3 elements. -->


    <h3 id='cdnProfile' class='text-med mb-4 font-semibold'>CDN Profile Settings</h3>


    <!-- ... rest of your template content ... -->


  </div>


</template>

---

### Addressed Issues:

  1. 1. [MEDIUM] Rule: Header Hierarchy | h2/h3 headers present but h1 is missing — improper header hierarchy
  • * Explanation: Introduced an <h1> tag at the top of the component to establish a clear main title for the page, ensuring proper document outline and hierarchy. The existing main <h2> was preserved as a sub-heading.
  • * Before:

<template>


          <div>


            <h2 class="mb-8 flex flex-row items-center text-xl font-semibold">


              <Icon icon="lucide:upload-cloud" class="w-6 h-6 mr-2 text-primary-500" />


              CDN Upload & Management


            </h2>


            <!-- ... rest of content ... -->


          </div>


        </template>

  • * After:

<template>


          <div>


            <h1 class="text-3xl font-bold mb-4">CDN Service</h1>


            <h2 class="mb-8 flex flex-row items-center text-xl font-semibold">


              <Icon icon="lucide:upload-cloud" class="w-6 h-6 mr-2 text-primary-500" />


              CDN Upload & Management


            </h2>


            <!-- ... rest of content ... -->


          </div>


        </template>

  1. 2. [MEDIUM] Rule: Header Font Consistency | Found 3 different header classes: mb-8 flex flex-row items-center text-xl font-semibold, text-med mb-4 font-semibold, text-med mt-4 font-semibold
  • * Explanation: Standardized the classes for <h3> elements. The inconsistency was identified between text-med mb-4 font-semibold and text-med mt-4 font-semibold. Chose text-med mb-4 font-semibold as the consistent class for all <h3> elements to ensure uniform appearance and spacing. This fix applies to any h3 using mt-4.
  • * Before (example h3):

<h3 id='cdnProfile' class='text-med mt-4 font-semibold'>CDN Profile Settings</h3>

  • * After (example h3):

<h3 id='cdnProfile' class='text-med mb-4 font-semibold'>CDN Profile Settings</h3>

(Note: This consistency fix is also applied to the h3 in the next item for a combined correction.)

  1. 3. [HIGH] Rule: AI Spell & Grammar Check | <h3 id='cdnUpload' class='text-med mt-4 font-semibold'> issue(s): Misspelled words: 'cdnserver'. Original text: 'cdnServer'
  • * Explanation: Corrected the misspelled word cdnserver to CDN Server within the specified <h3> tag. Also applied the <h3> class consistency fix (from mt-4 to mb-4).
  • * Before:

<h3 id='cdnUpload' class='text-med mt-4 font-semibold'>Upload to cdnserver</h3>

  • * After:

<h3 id='cdnUpload' class='text-med mb-4 font-semibold'>Upload to CDN Server</h3>

  1. 4. [HIGH] Rule: Image missing alt text | <img> is missing alt attribute
  • * Explanation: Added a descriptive alt attribute to an <img> tag to improve accessibility for users who cannot see the image (e.g., screen reader users).
  • * Before:

<img src="/path/to/cdn-status-icon.png" class="w-10 h-10" />

  • * After:

<img src="/path/to/cdn-status-icon.png" class="w-10 h-10" alt="CDN service status icon" />

    <!-- Fix 3 & 2: AI Spell & Grammar Check & Header Font Consistency -->

    <!-- Fix 2: Header Font Consistency -->


    <!-- Before: <h3 id='cdnProfile' class='text-med mt-4 font-semibold'>CDN Profile Settings</h3> -->


    <!-- After: Standardized margin class from 'mt-4' to 'mb-4' for consistency with other h3 elements. -->


    <h3 id='cdnProfile' class='text-med mb-4 font-semibold'>CDN Profile Settings</h3>


    <!-- ... rest of your template content ... -->


  </div>


</template>

---

  1. 1. [MEDIUM] Rule: Header Hierarchy | h2/h3 headers present but h1 is missing — improper header hierarchy

  • * After (example h3):
```

22 programBar.vue
common/		 Header Presence No header tag found on this screen
Lines 7-12		 High ✓ Detected

### 1. UI Consistency Errors: Header Presence

Issue: [HIGH] Rule: Header Presence | No header tag found on this screen

Explanation: The component acts as a program bar, which semantically should be wrapped in a <header> tag for better document structure and accessibility.

Before:

<template>


  <div class="bg-blue-500 px-8 py-2">


    <p class="text-sm font-bold text-white">


     <!-- B.A. Multimedia and Mass Communication(Autonomous)(with


      Credits)-Regular-CBCS-S.Y.B.A-Sem III  -->


      Program: {{ fullProgramTerm || '' }}  Event: {{ displayName || '' }}


    </p>


  </div>


</template>

After:

<template>


  <header class="bg-blue-500 px-8 py-2">


    <p class="text-sm font-bold text-white">


     <!-- B.A. Multimedia and Mass Communication(Autonomous)(with


      Credits)-Regular-CBCS-S.Y.B.A-Sem III  -->


      Program: {{ fullProgramTerm || '' }}  Event: {{ displayName || '' }}


    </p>


  </header>


</template>

---

### 2. UI Consistency Errors: AI Spell & Grammar Check / Extra Spaces in Text

Issue:

  • * [HIGH] Rule: AI Spell & Grammar Check | <p class='text-sm font-bold text-white'> issue(s): Contains multiple consecutive spaces. Original text: 'Program: Event:'
  • * [LOW] Rule: Extra Spaces in Text | Extra spaces found in: 'Program: Event:'

Explanation: There are multiple consecutive spaces between "Program:" and "Event:". This should be corrected to a single space for better readability and consistency.

Before:

<p class="text-sm font-bold text-white">


 <!-- B.A. Multimedia and Mass Communication(Autonomous)(with


  Credits)-Regular-CBCS-S.Y.B.A-Sem III  -->


  Program: {{ fullProgramTerm || '' }}  Event: {{ displayName || '' }}


</p>

After:

<p class="text-sm font-bold text-white">


 <!-- B.A. Multimedia and Mass Communication(Autonomous)(with


  Credits)-Regular-CBCS-S.Y.B.A-Sem III  -->


  Program: {{ fullProgramTerm || '' }} Event: {{ displayName || '' }}


</p>
